_notes: Hand-curated by reading the US Core Procedure differential (6 elements) line by line against the R4 Procedure snapshot. Key R4 facts: status min=1 required:event-status|4.0.1 (version-pinned); code min=0 example:procedure-code; subject min=1 Reference(Patient|Group); basedOn min=0 Reference(CarePlan|ServiceRequest); performed[x] min=0. Root constraint us-core-7 (severity=error): Performed SHALL be present if the status is 'completed' or 'in-progress'. USCDI annotation extensions present on basedOn, status, code, subject, performed[x].

drifts.description: Constraint 'us-core-7' added (severity: error): Performed SHALL be present if the status is 'completed' or 'in-progress'. Not present in R4 base Procedure.
Reference target profiles constrained: R4 allows Reference(CarePlan|ServiceRequest). US Core restricts to Reference(us-core-careplan|us-core-servicerequest). MustSupport is NOT added on this element.
Element-level USCDI requirement annotation extension (uscdi-requirement=true) added to Procedure.basedOn in target profile. Marks this element as a US Core Data for Interoperability requirement.
MustSupport added in target (min=1 in R4 -> conformance-critical).
Binding version pin removed: R4 binds to 'event-status|4.0.1' (version-locked). US Core binds to 'event-status' (no version pin). Strength remains required in both.
Element-level USCDI requirement annotation extension (uscdi-requirement=true) added to Procedure.status in target profile. Marks this element as a US Core Data for Interoperability requirement.
Cardinality changed: [0..1] -> [1..1]. Procedure code becomes mandatory in US Core; R4 allows a Procedure without a code.
MustSupport added in target (min becomes 1 -> conformance-critical).

Binding changed: R4 uses example strength to http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/procedure-code. US Core tightens to extensible strength with http://hl7.org/fhir/us/core/ValueSet/us-core-procedure-code (US-specific procedure codes including CPT, SNOMED CT). Binding strength tightened: example -> extensible.
Element-level USCDI requirement annotation extension (uscdi-requirement=true) added to Procedure.code in target profile. Marks this element as a US Core Data for Interoperability requirement.
MustSupport added in target (min=1 in R4 -> conformance-critical).
Reference target profile constrained: R4 allows Reference(Patient|Group). US Core restricts to Reference(us-core-patient) only.
Element-level USCDI requirement annotation extension (uscdi-requirement=true) added to Procedure.subject in target profile. Marks this element as a US Core Data for Interoperability requirement.
MustSupport added in target (min=0, optional but must be supported when present). Note: constraint us-core-7 conditionally requires it at runtime when status is 'completed' or 'in-progress'.
Element-level USCDI requirement annotation extension (uscdi-requirement=true) added to Procedure.performed[x] in target profile. Marks this element as a US Core Data for Interoperability requirement.
